g.data-narration: Welcome to Public Cloud Platforms! Today we explore GAE, AWS, and Azure - the three giants of cloud computing.
g.text: Public Cloud Platforms: GAE, AWS & Azure

g.data-narration: Public cloud platforms provide on-demand computing resources over the internet. They offer massive scalability with pay-as-you-go pricing.
g.text: • On-demand computing resources over the internet
• Operated by third-party providers (Google, Amazon, Microsoft)

g.data-narration: Understanding service models is crucial: IaaS gives virtual machines, PaaS provides platforms, SaaS delivers software applications.
Google App Engine (GAE) is a Platform as a Service offering launched in 2008. It completely abstracts away infrastructure management.
GAE automatically scales instances based on traffic and can scale to zero. It provides built-in services and supports multiple languages.
GAE has two environments: Standard is sandboxed with fast startup, Flexible runs Docker containers with more customization.
g.text: Sandboxed • Fast scale-up • Scales to zero
Docker containers • Any language • SSH access

g.data-narration: The developer deploys code via SDK, GAE's frontend distributes requests across auto-scaled instances, with built-in services below.
GAE provides key built-in services: Datastore for NoSQL storage, Memcache for caching, Task Queues for async processing.
The GAE deployment lifecycle: Develop with SDK, test locally, configure app.yaml, deploy with gcloud, then auto-scale in production.
g.text: Use SDK → app.yaml → gcloud deploy → Auto-scaling

g.data-narration: Amazon Web Services launched in 2006 is the market leader with 200+ services spanning IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS. Flagship: EC2, Lambda, S3.
AWS core services: EC2 for virtual servers (IaaS), Lambda for serverless functions, S3 for object storage, RDS for managed databases.
Microsoft Azure launched in 2010 is the second-largest cloud platform, excelling in hybrid cloud and enterprise integration with Microsoft ecosystem.
Azure core services map to AWS: VMs match EC2, Azure Functions match Lambda, Blob Storage matches S3, Cosmos DB matches DynamoDB.
Compare: GAE is PaaS, AWS offers IaaS/PaaS/SaaS, Azure focuses on hybrid. Key differences in management, scaling, and pricing.
g.text: Comparative Analysis: GAE vs AWS vs Azure

g.data-narration: Key difference: GAE (PaaS) manages code only with no SSH access, while AWS EC2 (IaaS) gives full OS control with root access.
g.text: Key Difference: PaaS (GAE) vs IaaS (AWS EC2)
Manage: Code only • No SSH • Zero maintenance
Manage: OS, middleware, runtime • Full SSH access
Best for GAE: Web apps, APIs, microservices (rapid deployment)
Best for EC2: Custom workloads, legacy apps, full control needed

g.data-narration: Know the service equivalents: Datastore ~ DynamoDB ~ Cosmos DB; S3 ~ Blob Storage ~ Cloud Storage; Lambda ~ Azure Functions.
